executable.title: GradientAD_sramtilak
executable.description: Applies gradient anisotropic diffusion (itk::GradientAnisotropicDiffusionImageFilter) to a 3D float image. Anisotropic diffusion smooths homogeneous regions while preserving edges by using the image gradient magnitude to modulate diffusion strength. Lower conductance values concentrate smoothing away from edges. More iterations increase overall smoothing
executable.acknowledgements: ITK GradientAnisotropicDiffusionImageFilter. ITK Software Guide Book 2, Chapter 3.

parameters.label: Input/Output
parameters.description: Input and output volume file paths

image.label: Input Volume
image.description: 3D input image to be filtered by gradient anisotropic diffusion.

image.label: Output Volume
image.description: Gradient anisotropic diffusion filtered 3D output image.

parameters.label: Filter Parameters
parameters.description: Controls for diffusion strength, stability, and number of update steps

double.label: Conductance
double.description: Controls sensitivity to edges. Lower values (0.5-1.0) preserve edges more strongly by inhibiting diffusion across high-gradient boundaries. Higher values (3.0+) allow more diffusion across edges, reducing noise more aggressively at the cost of edge blurring.

double.label: Time Step
double.description: Numerical integration time step. Must satisfy stability criterion (timeStep <= 0.125 for 3D). Smaller values are more stable but require more iterations to achieve the same smoothing effect.

integer.label: Iterations
integer.description: Number of diffusion update steps. More iterations produce stronger smoothing. Typical values are 5-20 for moderate denoising.
